zh.Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, England: 英國坎布利其爾布勒 格林
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh於1363年出生在英國坎布利其爾。她於1379年與John Ingelthorpe III在英國坎布利其爾布勒 格林結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1421年在她的家鄉享年58歲。

zh-hans.Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, England: 英国坎布利其爾布勒 格林
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh于1363年出生在英国坎布利其爾。她于1379年与John Ingelthorpe III在英国坎布利其爾布勒 格林结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1421年在她的家乡享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh was born in 1363, in Cambridgeshire, England. She married John Ingelthorpe III in 1379, in Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1421, in her hometown, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh est née en 1363, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John Ingelthorpe III en 1379, à Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1421, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh wurde 1363 in Cambridgeshire, England geboren. Sie hat 1379 in Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, England John Ingelthorpe III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1421 im Alter von 58 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh è nata il nel 1363 nel Cambridgeshire, England. Lei sposato John Ingelthorpe III nel 1379, nel Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1421, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.Burgh Green, Cambridgeshire, England: イギリスカンブリジェシュアイア
ja.Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, England: イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアブロウ グレン
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burghは1363年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアで生まれた。1379年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアブロウ グレンでJohn Ingelthorpe IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1421年に故郷で58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh(은)는 1363년 영국 캄브리지실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1379년 영국 캄브리지실르 버루그h 그린에서 John Ingelthorpe III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1421년 그녀의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh nasceu em 1363, em Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John Ingelthorpe III em 1379, em Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1421, em sua cidade natal, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh родилась в 1363 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Ingelthorpe III в 1379 г. в местности бурроую грин, камбриджешир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1421 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh nació en 1363, en Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con John Ingelthorpe III en 1379, en Burrough Green, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1421, en su pueblo, a la edad de 58 años.
